title: enneapogon asperatus
intro: Enneapogon asperatus, a species of grass in the family Poaceae, is a perennial plant that is native to Australia and is commonly known as rough grass. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Enneapogon asperatus has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its preparations and dosages should be carefully studied and supervised by a medical professional.
